title: Lupulus lupuloides ulmifolius
intro: Lupulus lupuloides ulmifolius, also known as the hop tree, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the family Salicaceae. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Lupulus lupuloides ulmifolius include flavonoids, tannins, and salicylic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
taxonomy: Lupulus lupuloides ulmifolius is classified within the genus Lupulus, which is a part of the family Cannabaceae. However, it is also sometimes classified as part of the family Rosaceae. Further research is needed to determine the most accurate classification. It is often considered a synonym for Lupulus angustifolius. The classification of this species is not universally agreed upon, and more studies are required to determine its correct classification.
